title: What is JavaScript?
description: Select the correct definition of JavaScript.
correct_answer: A high-level, interpreted programming language

title: What are the data types in JavaScript?
description: Which of the following is NOT a JavaScript data type?
correct_answer: Float

title: What is the typeof operator?
description: What does typeof return for an array?
correct_answer: object

title: What is a variable?
description: What is a variable in JavaScript?
options: A named container for storing data values
correct_answer: A named container for storing data values

title: What is the difference between let and var?
options: let is block-scoped; var is function-scoped
correct_answer: let is block-scoped; var is function-scoped

title: What is const used for?
description: What does the const keyword do?
options: Declares a variable that cannot be reassigned
Declares a variable that can change
correct_answer: Declares a variable that cannot be reassigned

title: What is an array?
description: How do you create an array in JavaScript?
correct_answer: const arr = [1, 2, 3]

title: What is an object in JavaScript?
description: Select the correct way to create an object.
correct_answer: const obj = { key: 'value' }

title: What is a function?
description: What is a function in JavaScript?
options: A reusable block of code that performs a task
correct_answer: A reusable block of code that performs a task

title: What is console.log?
options: Outputs a message to the web console
correct_answer: Outputs a message to the web console

title: What is the === operator?
correct_answer: Strict equality (value and type)

title: What is NaN?
correct_answer: Not a Number

title: What is null in JavaScript?
options: An intentional absence of any value
correct_answer: An intentional absence of any value

title: What is undefined?
options: When declared but not assigned a value
correct_answer: When declared but not assigned a value

title: What is a string?
description: How do you create a string in JavaScript?
correct_answer: Using quotes: 'hello' or "hello" or `hello`

title: What is template literal?
description: How do you use template literals?
correct_answer: Using backticks: `Hello ${name}`

title: What is an arrow function?
description: Select the correct arrow function syntax.
correct_answer: const fn = () => {}

title: What does Array.push() do?
description: What is the purpose of push()?
options: Adds elements to the end of an array
correct_answer: Adds elements to the end of an array

title: What does Array.pop() do?
options: Removes and returns the last element
correct_answer: Removes and returns the last element

title: What is a for loop?
description: What does a for loop do?
options: Repeats a block of code a specified number of times
correct_answer: Repeats a block of code a specified number of times

title: What is the while loop?
description: When does a while loop stop?
correct_answer: When the condition becomes false

title: What is an if statement?
description: What does an if statement do?
options: Executes code based on a condition
correct_answer: Executes code based on a condition

title: What is the ternary operator?
options: if condition then trueValue else falseValue
correct_answer: condition ? trueValue : falseValue

title: What is JSON?
correct_answer: JavaScript Object Notation

title: What does JSON.parse() do?
description: What is the purpose of JSON.parse()?
options: Converts a JSON string to a JavaScript object
Converts an object to a string
correct_answer: Converts a JSON string to a JavaScript object

title: What does JSON.stringify() do?
description: What is the output of JSON.stringify()?
options: A JSON string representation of a value
correct_answer: A JSON string representation of a value

title: What is the DOM?
correct_answer: Document Object Model

title: What is an event in JavaScript?
options: An action that occurs in the browser that JS can respond to
correct_answer: An action that occurs in the browser that JS can respond to

title: What is addEventListener?
options: Attaches an event handler to an element
correct_answer: Attaches an event handler to an element

title: What is the window object?

options: The browser window containing the DOM
correct_answer: The browser window containing the DOM

title: What is document.getElementById?
options: The element with the specified ID
correct_answer: The element with the specified ID

title: What is Math.random()?
correct_answer: A random number between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive)

title: What is the length property?
options: The number of elements in the array
correct_answer: The number of elements in the array

title: What does String.includes() do?
options: Whether a string contains a specified substring
correct_answer: Whether a string contains a specified substring

title: What is the switch statement?
options: When comparing a single value against multiple possible cases
correct_answer: When comparing a single value against multiple possible cases

title: What does Array.indexOf() return?
description: What does indexOf return if the element is not found?

title: What is the map() method?
options: Creates a new array by transforming each element
correct_answer: Creates a new array by transforming each element

title: What is the filter() method?
options: Creates a new array with elements that pass a test
correct_answer: Creates a new array with elements that pass a test

correct_answer: Handles errors in code execution

title: What is setTimeout?

title: What is setInterval?

correct_answer: Repeatedly executes a function at fixed time intervals

title: What is a boolean?
description: What are the possible boolean values?
correct_answer: true and false

title: What is the spread operator?

correct_answer: Expands an iterable into individual elements

title: What is destructuring?

correct_answer: Unpacks values from arrays or properties from objects into variables

A function that returns a number

options: Collects remaining arguments into an array

correct_answer: Reduces an array to a single accumulated value

correct_answer: Splits a string into an array of substrings

correct_answer: Joins array elements into a string

title: What is a class in JavaScript?
correct_answer: A template for creating objects

title: What is the this keyword?
description: What does 'this' refer to in a method?
options: The object the method belongs to

title: What is a module in JavaScript?
options: A file containing reusable code that can be imported/exported
correct_answer: A file containing reusable code that can be imported/exported

title: What is import/export?

correct_answer: export function myFunc() {} or export default myFunc

correct_answer: Executes a function for each array element

correct_answer: Removes whitespace from both ends of a string

correct_answer: Checks whether a value is an array

title: What is Object.values()?

correct_answer: An array of the object's own property values

title: What is the optional chaining operator?

correct_answer: Safely accesses nested object properties, returning undefined if any reference is nullish

title: What is the nullish coalescing operator?

Returns the right-hand when left is falsy

options: The first element that satisfies the test
correct_answer: The first element that satisfies the test

correct_answer: Whether at least one element passes the test

options: Whether all elements pass the test

options: Creates a new array with sub-array elements flattened
correct_answer: Creates a new array with sub-array elements flattened

options: The current date as a string
correct_answer: Milliseconds since January 1, 1970

title: What is scope in JavaScript?
correct_answer: The accessibility/visibility of variables

title: What is hoisting?
options: Variable and function declarations are moved to the top of their scope
correct_answer: Variable and function declarations are moved to the top of their scope

title: What is String.toUpperCase()?
options: Converts a string to uppercase letters
correct_answer: Converts a string to uppercase letters

options: Extracts a section of the string and returns it as a new string
correct_answer: Extracts a section of the string and returns it as a new string

correct_answer: Merges two or more arrays into a new array

title: What is the typeof null?
correct_answer: object

title: What are default parameters?
description: How do you set a default parameter value?

options: Reverses the order of elements in place

options: A unique and immutable primitive value
correct_answer: A unique and immutable primitive value

title: What is typeof undefined?
correct_answer: undefined

options: Values of iterable objects (arrays, strings, etc.)
correct_answer: Values of iterable objects (arrays, strings, etc.)

title: What is the for...in loop?

Whether a value is in an array

Whether a number is in range

correct_answer: h

title: What is a closure?

options: A function that remembers its outer scope even after the outer function has returned
correct_answer: A function that remembers its outer scope even after the outer function has returned

title: What is the event loop?

options: Processes the call stack and message queue, executing callbacks when the stack is empty
Loops through events in the DOM
correct_answer: Processes the call stack and message queue, executing callbacks when the stack is empty

title: What is a Promise?
options: A value that may be available now, in the future, or never
correct_answer: A value that may be available now, in the future, or never

title: What is async/await?

A new way to create loops

title: What is the prototype chain?

correct_answer: Objects inherit properties and methods from their prototype, forming a chain

title: What is the call stack?

title: What is the difference between == and ===?
options: == performs type coercion; === checks type and value strictly
correct_answer: == performs type coercion; === checks type and value strictly

title: What is debouncing?
options: Delays execution until a pause in rapid calls
correct_answer: Delays execution until a pause in rapid calls

title: What is throttling?
description: How does throttling differ from debouncing?
options: Throttling limits execution to once per time interval; debouncing waits for inactivity
correct_answer: Throttling limits execution to once per time interval; debouncing waits for inactivity

title: What is event delegation?
options: Attaching a single event listener to a parent to handle events from children via bubbling
correct_answer: Attaching a single event listener to a parent to handle events from children via bubbling

title: What is event bubbling?
options: The event propagates from the target up to the root
The event goes from root to target
correct_answer: The event propagates from the target up to the root

title: What is event capturing?
options: The event propagates from the root down to the target
The event goes from target to root
correct_answer: The event propagates from the root down to the target

title: What is the Fetch API?
options: A Promise that resolves to the Response object
correct_answer: A Promise that resolves to the Response object

title: What is IIFE?

options: A function that runs as soon as it is defined: (function() {})()
correct_answer: A function that runs as soon as it is defined: (function() {})()

title: What is the difference between null and undefined?
options: null is an intentional empty value; undefined means a variable hasn't been assigned
null is for numbers; undefined is for strings

title: What is a generator function?

correct_answer: An object with a next() method that returns { value, done }

title: What is Reflect in JavaScript?

WeakMap is the same as Map

correct_answer: Creates a new function with a fixed this value

options: Creates a new object with the specified prototype

description: What does the module pattern achieve?
options: Encapsulation by using closures to create private scope
correct_answer: Encapsulation by using closures to create private scope

title: What is the difference between shallow and deep copy?
options: Shallow copy copies references; deep copy copies all nested values
Deep copy only copies the first level
correct_answer: Shallow copy copies references; deep copy copies all nested values

title: How to deep clone an object?
description: What is a reliable way to deep clone?

correct_answer: structuredClone() or JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj)) for simple objects

title: What is the MutationObserver?
options: Watches for changes to the DOM tree

options: When an element enters or exits the viewport

correct_answer: localStorage has larger capacity (~5MB) and isn't sent with every HTTP request; cookies are sent with requests

options: When the first promise settles (resolves or rejects)

title: What is Promise.allSettled()?

title: What is Promise.any()?
correct_answer: When any promise fulfills successfully

title: What is the microtask queue?
description: How do microtasks differ from macrotasks?
options: Microtasks (Promise callbacks) run before the next macrotask (setTimeout)
correct_answer: Microtasks (Promise callbacks) run before the next macrotask (setTimeout)

title: What is the requestAnimationFrame?

options: Before the next repaint, typically at 60fps
correct_answer: Before the next repaint, typically at 60fps

title: What is a tagged template literal?
options: A function called with a template literal, receiving strings and values as arguments
correct_answer: A function called with a template literal, receiving strings and values as arguments

title: What is the globalThis object?
options: A standard way to access the global object across environments
correct_answer: A standard way to access the global object across environments

title: What is structuredClone()?

correct_answer: Creates a deep clone of a value using the structured clone algorithm

correct_answer: Allows you to abort web requests (fetch) and other async operations

title: What are private class fields?
description: How do you declare a private field?
correct_answer: Using the # prefix: #privateField

title: What are static class methods?
options: A method called on the class itself, not on instances
correct_answer: A method called on the class itself, not on instances

title: What is the for await...of loop?
description: When do you use for await...of?
correct_answer: To iterate over async iterables

title: What is a SharedArrayBuffer?
options: Sharing memory between the main thread and Web Workers
correct_answer: Sharing memory between the main thread and Web Workers

title: What is optional catch binding?

options: You can omit the error parameter in catch: catch { }
correct_answer: You can omit the error parameter in catch: catch { }

title: What is the at() method?
options: The last element of the array
correct_answer: The last element of the array

title: What is Object.hasOwn()?

options: Object.hasOwn is a static method that works on objects that don't inherit from Object.prototype
correct_answer: Object.hasOwn is a static method that works on objects that don't inherit from Object.prototype

title: What is Array.groupBy()?
options: Groups array elements by a criterion into an object
correct_answer: Groups array elements by a criterion into an object

title: What is the top-level await?
options: Using await at the module top level without wrapping in an async function
correct_answer: Using await at the module top level without wrapping in an async function

title: What is a memory leak in JavaScript?
options: Unintentionally retaining references to objects that are no longer needed
correct_answer: Unintentionally retaining references to objects that are no longer needed

title: What is the temporal dead zone?
options: The period between entering a scope and the variable's declaration where let/const cannot be accessed
correct_answer: The period between entering a scope and the variable's declaration where let/const cannot be accessed

title: Explain the execution context.
description: What is an execution context in JavaScript?
options: The environment in which JS code is evaluated, including variable/scope chain/this binding
correct_answer: The environment in which JS code is evaluated, including variable/scope chain/this binding

title: How does garbage collection work in V8?
options: Generational GC with Scavenger (young gen) and Mark-Sweep-Compact (old gen)
correct_answer: Generational GC with Scavenger (young gen) and Mark-Sweep-Compact (old gen)

title: What is the difference between V8 hidden classes and inline caches?
description: How do they optimize property access?
options: Hidden classes provide structure; inline caches cache property lookup locations for fast access
correct_answer: Hidden classes provide structure; inline caches cache property lookup locations for fast access

title: What is tail call optimization?
options: Reuses the current stack frame for tail-position calls, preventing stack overflow
correct_answer: Reuses the current stack frame for tail-position calls, preventing stack overflow

title: What is the Realm in JavaScript?
options: A distinct global environment with its own set of built-in objects
correct_answer: A distinct global environment with its own set of built-in objects

title: What is the Atomics object?
options: Atomic operations on SharedArrayBuffer for thread-safe programming
correct_answer: Atomic operations on SharedArrayBuffer for thread-safe programming

title: How does the JavaScript JIT compiler work?
options: Compiles hot code paths to machine code at runtime for faster execution
correct_answer: Compiles hot code paths to machine code at runtime for faster execution

title: What is the spec-level difference between function declarations and expressions?
options: Declarations are hoisted completely; expressions are hoisted as undefined variables
correct_answer: Declarations are hoisted completely; expressions are hoisted as undefined variables

title: What are the internal slots of a Promise?
description: What internal state does a Promise have?
correct_answer: [[PromiseState]] (pending/fulfilled/rejected) and [[PromiseResult]]

title: How does the event loop prioritize tasks?
options: Call stack → Microtask queue (Promises) → Macrotask queue (setTimeout, I/O)
correct_answer: Call stack → Microtask queue (Promises) → Macrotask queue (setTimeout, I/O)

title: What is zone.js and how does it work?
options: Monkey-patches async APIs to create execution contexts for tracking async operations
correct_answer: Monkey-patches async APIs to create execution contexts for tracking async operations

title: Explain the spec difference between [[Call]] and [[Construct]].
options: [[Call]] when invoked normally; [[Construct]] when invoked with new. Arrow functions lack [[Construct]]
correct_answer: [[Call]] when invoked normally; [[Construct]] when invoked with new. Arrow functions lack [[Construct]]

title: What is the Abstract Equality Comparison algorithm?
options: Applies type coercion rules: ToNumber for comparisons with numbers, ToPrimitive for objects
correct_answer: Applies type coercion rules: ToNumber for comparisons with numbers, ToPrimitive for objects

title: What are weak references in JavaScript?
options: Holds a weak reference to an object that doesn't prevent garbage collection
correct_answer: Holds a weak reference to an object that doesn't prevent garbage collection

title: What is the FinalizationRegistry?
options: Registers a callback to be called when a registered object is garbage collected
correct_answer: Registers a callback to be called when a registered object is garbage collected

title: How does V8 optimize polymorphic call sites?
description: What happens when a function receives different shapes of objects?
options: V8 uses inline caches (monomorphic → polymorphic → megamorphic), deoptimizing at megamorphic
correct_answer: V8 uses inline caches (monomorphic → polymorphic → megamorphic), deoptimizing at megamorphic

title: What are the phases of the TC39 proposal process?
description: How many stages does a proposal go through?
correct_answer: Stage 0 (Strawperson) → Stage 1 (Proposal) → Stage 2 (Draft) → Stage 3 (Candidate) → Stage 4 (Finished)

title: How does the Structured Clone algorithm differ from JSON serialization?
description: What can structuredClone handle that JSON cannot?
options: Circular references, Date, RegExp, Map, Set, ArrayBuffer, Blob, and more
correct_answer: Circular references, Date, RegExp, Map, Set, ArrayBuffer, Blob, and more

title: What is import.meta?
options: Context-specific metadata about the current module, like import.meta.url
A way to import all modules
correct_answer: Context-specific metadata about the current module, like import.meta.url

title: What is the Iterator Helpers proposal?
options: Lazy methods like map, filter, take on iterators without converting to arrays
correct_answer: Lazy methods like map, filter, take on iterators without converting to arrays

title: Implement a debounce function.
description: Write a debounce(fn, delay) function that delays invoking fn until after delay ms have elapsed since the last call. Return the debounced function.
correct_answer: function debounce(fn, delay) { let timer; return function(...args) { clearTimeout(timer); timer = setTimeout(() => fn.apply(this, args), delay); }; }

title: Implement a throttle function.
description: Write a throttle(fn, limit) function that ensures fn is called at most once every limit milliseconds.
correct_answer: function throttle(fn, limit) { let inThrottle = false; return function(...args) { if (!inThrottle) { fn.apply(this, args); inThrottle = true; setTimeout(() => inThrottle = false, limit); } }; }

title: Implement Array.prototype.myMap.
description: Write a custom implementation of Array.map without using the built-in map method.
correct_answer: Array.prototype.myMap = function(callback) { const result = []; for (let i = 0; i < this.length; i++) { result.push(callback(this[i], i, this)); } return result; };

title: Implement a deep clone function.
description: Write a function deepClone(obj) that creates a deep copy of an object, handling nested objects and arrays. Handle basic types (no need for Date, RegExp, Map, Set).
correct_answer: function deepClone(obj) { if (obj === null || typeof obj !== 'object') return obj; const clone = Array.isArray(obj) ? [] : {}; for (const key in obj) { if (obj.hasOwnProperty(key)) { clone[key] = deepClone(obj[key]); } } return clone; }

title: Implement Promise.all from scratch.
description: Write a function myPromiseAll(promises) that works like Promise.all. It should resolve with an array of values when all promises resolve, or reject with the first rejection reason.
correct_answer: function myPromiseAll(promises) { return new Promise((resolve, reject) => { const results = []; let completed = 0; if (promises.length === 0) return resolve([]); promises.forEach((p, i) => { Promise.resolve(p).then(value => { results[i] = value; completed++; if (completed === promises.length) resolve(results); }).catch(reject); }); }); }

title: Implement a function currying utility.
description: Write a function curry(fn) that returns a curried version of fn. The curried function should collect arguments until enough are gathered to call the original function.
correct_answer: function curry(fn) { return function curried(...args) { if (args.length >= fn.length) { return fn.apply(this, args); } return function(...args2) { return curried.apply(this, args.concat(args2)); }; }; }

title: Implement a flat function for arrays.
description: Write a function flatten(arr, depth=1) that flattens a nested array up to the specified depth. Do not use Array.flat.
correct_answer: function flatten(arr, depth = 1) { const result = []; for (const item of arr) { if (Array.isArray(item) && depth > 0) { result.push(...flatten(item, depth - 1)); } else { result.push(item); } } return result; }

title: Implement an event emitter.
description: Create a class EventEmitter with methods on(event, cb), off(event, cb), and emit(event, ...args). on registers a callback, off removes it, emit triggers all callbacks for the event.
correct_answer: class EventEmitter { constructor() { this.events = {}; } on(event, cb) { (this.events[event] = this.events[event] || []).push(cb); } off(event, cb) { this.events[event] = (this.events[event] || []).filter(fn => fn !== cb); } emit(event, ...args) { (this.events[event] || []).forEach(fn => fn(...args)); } }

title: Implement memoize function.
description: Write a function memoize(fn) that returns a memoized version of fn. Cache results based on the stringified arguments.
correct_answer: function memoize(fn) { const cache = {}; return function(...args) { const key = JSON.stringify(args); if (key in cache) return cache[key]; return cache[key] = fn.apply(this, args); }; }

title: Implement a pipe function.
description: Write a function pipe(...fns) that returns a new function that passes its argument through each function from left to right.
correct_answer: function pipe(...fns) { return function(x) { return fns.reduce((acc, fn) => fn(acc), x); }; }
